item.title: Top 25 Fantasy Books According To Imgur
item.description:
25. The First Law Series - Joe Abercrombie 24. The Inheritance Cycle - Christopher Paolini 23. Monster Hunter International - Larry Correia 22. The Gentlemen Bastards - Scott Lynch
21. Clan Of The Cave Bears - Jean M. Auel 20. PowderMage Trilogy - Brian McLellan 19. The Lightbringer Series - Brent Weeks 18. The Greenbone Saga - Fonda Lee
17. Sandman Slim - Richard Kadrey 16. Malazan Book Of The Fallen - Steven Erikson 15. Mistborn - Brandon Sanderson 14. The Band Series - Nicholas Eames
13. The Furies Of Calderon - Jim Butcher 12. His Dark Materials - Philip Pullman 11. The Dark Tower - Stephen King 10. The Farseer Trilogy - Robin Hobb
9. The Wheel of Time - Robert Jordan 8. The KingKiller Chronicles - Patrick Rothfuss 7. Harry Potter - JK Rowling 6. Dragonriders of Pern - Anne McCaffrey
5. The Way of Kings - Brandon Sanderson 4. The Dresden Files - Jim butcher 3. American Gods - Neil Gaiman 2. Discworld - Terry Pratchett
1. Good Omens - Terry Pratchett & Neil Gaiman Thank you all for for commenting on my last book post, it was a lot of work going through all 1408 comments and telling them up. https://imgur.com/gallery/gteu1BK Here is the sci-fi list https://imgur.com/gallery/Sn13wD9
item.dc:creator: Beattywill80

item.title: When you acquire tag alone friends
item.dc:creator: EnderTheTurk

item.title: My fiancé up and fucking died.
item.description: I just need to vent this and I dont want to make my friends and family nervous. My fiance died and I can't stop thinking about the life we're both missing out on now. We were going to buy a house this month. We were talking about finally having space and money to maybe have kids. We were supposed to be at Disney this week. I told him to wait till we had been dating 4 years before he proposed because I'd read there was a noted uptick in breakups around 4 years when people begin to realize the things that are permanent about a person. But after 4 years, I couldn't find anything about him that I didn't like. We'd only been engaged for a year. Why rush? I knew I wanted him he knew he wanted me. We weren't going anywhere. He would scoop up every bug in the house and put it outside. Even the ones he was really, deeply grossed out by. Spiders, moths, houseflies, those centipedes with the awful long legs. Even as his hands shook from being near the thing, he wouldn't kill it. Just catch it under a cup with piece of mail. This man was a Disney princess. Wild animals would come up to him. Birds would land on him. Stranger's children would come up and hold his hand and not even be alarmed to realize he was a stranger. I've never met someone with an aura. But he had one. And it made people feel safe. And it confused him a lot. Because he had anxiety often. And he made me feel strong. Plenty of dudes are happy to be your defender but he built me up so I could defend myself. He told me once "the stupidest thing anyone could do is underestimate you." And I told him that was the sexiest thing a man has ever said to me. Because it was. I was with a guy who made me feel like trash before him. That guy pushed me at a party once because he was drunk and insecure. My Fiance told me then, when we were just friends, that wasn't okay. Not that my bf was a monster or an asshole or I should leave him. I could argue with all of that. I couldn't argue with "that wasn't okay." Because it wasn't okay. He was my DnD DM first. He spun beautiful stories. Voices and puns and heartbreak and backstories that come back later. Varying cultures and history and maps and props. He was writing a dual campaign that would require 2 DMs and a huge group so the 2 groups could decide to fight or cooperate. He loved strategy board games that took 7 hours to play. He loved Star Trek and inventing gods and arguing philosophy but not in that annoying way where he just quotes other people's ideas that are supposed to be impressive. He had thoughts of his own that were complex and kind and sometimes dark with despair about how best intentions don't line up with real world ability. His despair was beautiful too. He'd say we should pray no God exists because, by evidence of existence, if it is not all random chance and cause and effect, God is either not kind or not competent. But humans at least want to be kind, we just get scared. When we disagreed, we knew how to respect each other anyway. Exactly once we had a real fight and we figured it out that night. I came out of the shower one day and he was just dead. He wasn't sick. He hadn't been injured or poisoned or overdosed. He didn't have chest pains. It's been a month and we haven't heard anything from the Coroner. I'm starting to worry all I'm going to get back are his ashes and a shrug. We had everything and then it disappeared. Maybe it's not cool to stunt on other widows or whatever but I mean we were *happier* than the average couple. We didn't fight AND we still were able to talk about important shit without being rude AND our sex life was great and getting better AND our savings plan was working. Part of me thinks about that scene in the princess bride, "you truly loved each other, so you might have even been truly happy. Not one couple in a thousand has that, not one. Why should you?" This will get lost in usersub and I want it to. I just need to put down the impressive, complex person he was. His family have very specific religious views and it keeps them from learning about who he really was. I dont know why, but that kills me. People should know who he was. And how fucking stupid it is that he just up and died all because I was in the shower for 5 minutes longer than he was. I want to die. But that won't bring him back and we were never people who believed we'd see each other when we died. So I'll muddle through. Because I promised him once that I would and because someone needs to tell people who he was.
item.dc:creator: KatieIsALady

item.title: Top 25 SciFi Novels According To Imgur
item.description:
25. Wayfarer Series - Beck Chambers 24. Magic 2.0 - Scott Meyer 23. Parable Of The Sower - Octavia Butler 22. Frankenstein - Mary Shelley
21. The Martian - Andy Weir 20. Armor - John Steakley 19. Arena Mode - Blake Northcott @BlakeNorthcott 18. The Bobiverse - Dennis E. Taylor
17. The Old Man's War - John Scalzi 16. Neuromancer - William Gibson 15. World War Z - Max Brooks 14. Altered Carbon - Richard K. Morgan
13. The Handmaid's Tale - Margaret Atwood 12. Ready Player One - Ernest Cline 11. Red Rising - Pierce Brown 10. The Murderbot Diaries - Martha Wells
9. Snow Crash - Neil Stephenson 8. 1984 - George Orwell 7. Flowers For Algernon - Daniel Keyes 6. Slaughterhouse-Five - Kurt Vonnegut
5. Dune - Frank Harbert 4. Project Hail Mary - Andy Weir 3. The Expanse - James A. Corey 2. Ender's Game - Orson Scott Card
1. The Hitchhiker's Guide To The Galaxy - Douglas Adams Thank you all for commenting on my book post, it was alot of work crawling through all 1408 comments and tallying up the winners above. Quite surprisingly a few of the books presented above overturned higher numbers due to repeated appearances at lower numbers. https://imgur.com/gallery/gteu1BK I'm working on the fantasy list I'll tag it here when I'm done. Edit: https://imgur.com/gallery/LZW9V85
item.dc:creator: Beattywill80

item.title: Samuel L. Jackson Finally Received His First-Ever Oscar
item.description:
No actor’s films have made more money in box office history than Samuel L Jackson, but he had never won an Oscar until Friday, when he was awarded an honorary statuette by the Academy. The 73-year-old known for collaborations with Spike Lee and Quentin Tarantino, and a regular feature in blockbusters from Marvel to Star Wars, had previously secured only one Oscar nomination for “Pulp Fiction.”
Jackson was presented with a statuette honoring career achievement at a star-studded Governors Awards gala in Hollywood, just two days before the main Academy Awards in which last year’s films and nominees will compete.
“152 movie titles, $27 billion in box office, more than any other actor in history,” said Denzel Washington, presenting Jackson with the award. Jackson, seated alongside Tarantino in the Dolby Ballroom, took the stage to reminisce on his career, which also includes the original “Jurassic Park” and action sequel “Die Hard with a Vengeance.” “It’s been a real pleasure making indelible impressions on audiences as ‘gang member number two,’ ‘hold-up man’ and ‘unforgettable black guy’ — just to name a few,” he joked.
Jackson wasn’t the only Hollywood icon to be recognized at the Governors Awards. Three other honorees received trophies at the event. Elaine May — a comedic trailblazer and acclaimed writer-director — and actor Liv Ullmann — frequent collaborator of filmmaker Ingmar Bergman — also received Honorary Awards. Likewise, actor Danny Glover received the Jean Hersholt Humanitarian Award. In his statement, Rubin credited Glover’s award to his “decades-long advocacy for justice and human rights reflects his dedication to recognizing our shared humanity on and off the screen.” Much like Jackson, none of these honorees ever won an Oscar before.
item.dc:creator: Yaratam

item.dc:creator: Ngugi

item.title: My best friend and godson were in a horrible accident
item.description: My best friend (since we were 6 months old & we’re now 34) and godson (8yo) were in a horrible accident last weekend. My godson has been on life support ever since, and the doctors want to take him off support in a day or two. We know he’s not going to survive it, and it feels unreal. I’m trying my best to be there for my her, but how does a mother say goodbye to their child? I keep thinking of all the things he’ll never do. I know this moment isn’t about me, but I just can’t believe this is happening, and I’m utterly heartbroken.
item.dc:creator: CaptCrunched

item.dc:creator: WilliamKeith

item.title: You can't prepare for this
item.description: My mom passed away yesterday from mesothelioma (lung cancer). The day before she went, she called me and my dad into the room and just held our hands. When my dad left, I asked her how she's feeling. She wheezed out, "I'm so tired". I'm pretty sure that was her saying she's going to stop fighting. I'm glad she's no longer suffering. I just miss my mom. Edit - Thanks everyone for the comforting messages. This is really a great community. All I can say is: love the people you love with everything you have. P.s. - I'm a chick not a dude ;-)
item.dc:creator: ManicallyDiscontent
